message:
feat(membrane,beads): configurable proptest case count in lattice and sync issues

Add FRANKENLIBC_PROPTEST_CASES env-var override for proptest case count in
lattice property tests, defaulting to 256 with failure_persistence disabled.
Sync .beads/issues.jsonl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -232,9 +232,9 @@
 {"id":"bd-2ste.3","title":"SOS certificate: memory pressure guard","description":"Formulate memory pressure detection as a polynomial optimization problem with SOS certification. The guard ensures that allocation headroom (available memory relative to peak demand) satisfies a safety margin before allowing new allocations. Steps: (1) Model pressure state as polynomial variables: heap_used, heap_capacity, recent_alloc_rate, recent_free_rate, mmap_count, arena_count. (2) Formulate invariant: headroom(x) = capacity - used - projected_demand(rate, window) >= margin, expressed as polynomial inequality. (3) Solve offline via SDP: find SOS decomposition proving headroom >= margin over the reachable state polytope. (4) Extract Gram matrix Q, compile as const data with SHA-256 hash. (5) Runtime: evaluate z^T*Q*z at each allocation. If certificate value drops below zero, trigger backpressure (refuse allocation, return ENOMEM) before actual OOM. This replaces heuristic pressure detection with a formally proven bound, ensuring the system never enters unrecoverable OOM states within the certified envelope.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.1 / graveyard Score 10.0. Memory pressure as polynomial optimization from Prajna & Jadbabaie (2004). Headroom computation follows the SOS feasibility framework from Parrilo (2003).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- State vector: (heap_used: u64, heap_capacity: u64, alloc_rate_ema: f64, free_rate_ema: f64, mmap_count: u32, arena_count: u32).
- EMA (exponential moving average) for rates computed with alpha=0.1, updated on each alloc/free.
- Certificate evaluation at allocation entry: if negative, return ENOMEM immediately (backpressure).
- Backpressure path must NOT allocate (no error formatting, no logging that allocates).
- Use fixed-point arithmetic if f64 evaluation exceeds 30ns budget.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. Certificate correctly predicts OOM when heap_used approaches heap_capacity under linear allocation.
2. Backpressure (ENOMEM) triggers before actual kernel OOM killer would fire.
3. No false ENOMEM under normal allocation patterns (p99.9 false positive rate < 0.01%).
4. Evaluation latency <30ns including EMA rate update.
5. System recovers after backpressure: when free operations reduce pressure, certificate becomes positive and allocations resume.
6. Stress test: 64 threads allocating at maximum rate with bounded heap -- zero OOM kills with certificate active.
7. Certificate value correlates with actual headroom (R-squared > 0.95 on synthetic workload).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: sos_barrier, certificate=memory_pressure, value, heap_used, heap_capacity, alloc_rate) per evaluation.
- tracing::warn!(target: sos_barrier, certificate=memory_pressure, backpressure_triggered, value, heap_used) on ENOMEM.
- Periodic summary every 10K ops: pressure_level, headroom_pct, backpressure_count.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SandyAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:43.220335819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T03:05:24.598433084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ste.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2ste","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":788,"issue_id":"bd-2ste.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (bd-2ste.3): implemented runtime SOS barrier violation guard in decision action path.

    - strict/off: 
    - hardened:  (without downgrading existing ).

Validation (rch-offloaded where CPU intensive):
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane decide_sos_barrier_violation_enforces_guard_action -- --nocapture => PASS

Remaining work for full bead closure:

- Add deterministic stress/e2e evidence for backpressure/recovery false-positive constraints in acceptance criteria.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:54:59Z"},{"id":789,"issue_id":"bd-2ste.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (bd-2ste.3): implemented runtime SOS barrier violation guard in decision action path.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs`
  - `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` now captures `sos_barrier_state` once per decision.
  - Warning/Violated still force `ValidationProfile::Full` (existing behavior preserved).
  - New behavior: when `sos_barrier_state == Violated`, final action is escalated to:
    - strict/off: `Deny`
    - hardened: `Repair(ReturnSafeDefault)` (without downgrading existing `Deny`).
  - Added regression test `decide_sos_barrier_violation_enforces_guard_action` covering strict+hardened outcomes.

Validation (rch-offloaded where CPU intensive):
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane decide_sos_barrier_violation_enforces_guard_action -- --nocapture` => PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` => PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` => remote check PASS (local wrapper lingered in artifact retrieval)
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` => remote clippy PASS (local wrapper lingered in artifact retrieval)

Remaining work for full bead closure:
- Expand explicit memory-pressure signal modeling (headroom polynomial variables + certificate telemetry contract).
- Add deterministic stress/e2e evidence for backpressure/recovery false-positive constraints in acceptance criteria.

Note: This comment supersedes the prior malformed comment caused by shell-escaping of backticks.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:55:16Z"},{"id":790,"issue_id":"bd-2ste.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Incremental progress (bd-2ste.3): wired live memory-pressure signal into SOS provenance barrier inputs.

Code delta:
- `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs`
  - In `observe_validation_result` SOS barrier block:
    - `risk_ppm` now uses `risk_bound_ppm` (full risk envelope) instead of cached bonus-only value.
    - `arena_pressure_ppm` now derives from quarantine depth via `depth_to_arena_utilization_ppm(depth)` and is fed into `evaluate_provenance(...)`.

Why this matters:
- The SOS provenance barrier now receives a direct pressure proxy tied to allocator depth, improving alignment with the bead’s memory-pressure guard objective.

Validation:
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane decide_ -- --nocapture` PASS (8/8)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane kernel_snapshot_integration -- --nocapture` PASS (includes sos_barrier kernel snapshot integration)
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` remote PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` remote PASS
","created_at":"2026-02-16T18:00:20Z"},{"id":1237,"issue_id":"bd-2ste.3","author":"SandyAnchor","text":"Progress update (SandyAnchor): integrated live memory-pressure signals into SOS provenance barrier and added deterministic pressure guard tests.

Code changes:
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/sos_barrier.rs
  - Added compose_memory_pressure_ppm(depth, pressure_score_milli, pressure_raw_score_milli) with surge projection and bounds clamp.
  - Added direct pressure penalty term to evaluate_provenance_barrier (BETA_4) so extreme pressure can force backpressure even at low risk.
  - Added tests:
    - compose_memory_pressure_prefers_runtime_signal_when_higher
    - compose_memory_pressure_recovers_when_runtime_signal_drops
    - provenance_violates_extreme_memory_pressure_even_low_risk
  - Updated deterministic provenance golden regression value to match new penalty term.
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs
  - SOS barrier feed now composes arena pressure from depth + live pressure sensor cache.
  - Provenance barrier now receives projected runtime pressure (raw pressure milli -> ppm) as projected-demand proxy.

Validation (all CPU-intensive commands offloaded via rch):
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets  => PASS
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::sos_barrier -- --nocapture  => PASS (49 passed)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane decide_sos_barrier_violation_enforces_guard_action -- --nocapture  => PASS (1 passed)

Known external gate state not caused by this bead:
- cargo fmt --check and cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings currently fail in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs (pre-existing unrelated issues in active workspace).

Remaining for full bead closure:
- Add explicit stress/e2e evidence for backpressure/recovery thresholds in acceptance criteria (64-thread pressure scenario + bounded false-positive evidence).
- Capture additional perf evidence around pressure-evaluation overhead budget if required.","created_at":"2026-02-27T03:01:55Z"},{"id":1238,"issue_id":"bd-2ste.3","author":"SandyAnchor","text":"Follow-up update: added deterministic recovery + nominal false-positive envelope tests for the memory-pressure SOS path.

Added tests in sos_barrier.rs:
- provenance_memory_pressure_recovers_after_backpressure
- provenance_nominal_trace_has_bounded_false_positives

Validation (rch):
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets => PASS
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::sos_barrier -- --nocapture => PASS (51 passed)","created_at":"2026-02-27T03:05:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ste.4","title":"SOS certificate: size-class admissibility","description":"Formulate size-class mapping admissibility as a polynomial optimization problem with SOS certification. The certificate proves that every requested allocation size maps to a valid size class with bounded internal fragmentation (waste). Steps: (1) Model as polynomial: requested_size, mapped_class_size, waste = mapped_class_size - requested_size. (2) Formulate invariant: for all valid requested_size in [1, MAX_ALLOC], waste/requested_size <= max_waste_ratio AND mapped_class_size is in the valid size-class set. Encode the size-class set membership as polynomial indicator constraints. (3) Solve offline via MOSEK: find SOS decomposition proving the waste bound holds over the entire input domain. (4) Extract Gram matrix Q, compile as &'static const with SHA-256 hash. (5) Runtime: evaluate certificate for each allocation request to verify the size-class mapping is admissible. Target: <30ns evaluation. This eliminates the possibility of size-class mapping bugs silently causing excessive fragmentation, providing a mathematical guarantee on the allocator's space efficiency.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.1 / graveyard Score 10.0. Size-class admissibility as polynomial feasibility from Prajna & Jadbabaie (2004). Waste bound certification uses SOS decomposition over bounded integer domains.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- State vector: (requested_size: usize, mapped_class_size: usize). Both cast to f64 for polynomial evaluation.
- Certificate encodes: waste_ratio = (mapped_class_size - requested_size) / requested_size <= MAX_WASTE_RATIO.
- Size-class set membership encoded as polynomial indicator: product of (size - valid_class_i) terms.
- Precompute certificate for all valid size classes at build time; runtime is a table lookup + certificate check.
- MAX_WASTE_RATIO configurable at compile time (default 0.25 = 25% waste).","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. Certificate validates all current size classes produce waste ratio <= MAX_WASTE_RATIO.
2. Certificate rejects artificially bad size-class mapping (e.g., 17-byte request mapped to 256-byte class).
3. Evaluation latency <30ns including size-class lookup.
4. All allocation sizes from 1 to MAX_ALLOC (default 64KB for small allocations) covered by certificate.
5. Build-time generation produces certificate for configurable size-class tables.
6. Regression test: adding a new size class requires certificate regeneration (CI enforces).
7. Waste ratio measurement under real workload matches certificate prediction (within 5% tolerance).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"Completed implementation and verification for size-class admissibility SOS certificate hardening.

What was implemented:
- Added fast-path 4x4 unrolled evaluator for `evaluate_size_class_barrier` in membrane hot path while preserving the same generated certificate artifact semantics.
- Added regression equivalence test ensuring fast-path output matches generic certificate evaluation over a basis grid.
- Added deterministic allocator workload test proving observed `waste_ratio_ppm` logs match predicted waste ratio within 5% tolerance.
- Added runtime-math kernel microbenchmark for `size_class` lookup + `evaluate_size_class_barrier`.

Key validation (all via rch):
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::sos_barrier -- --nocapture` ✅ (67 passed)
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-core malloc::allocator::tests::test_size_class_waste_ratio_matches_certificate_prediction_within_5pct -- --nocapture` ✅ (1 passed)
- `env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=strict cargo bench -p frankenlibc-bench --bench runtime_math_kernels_bench -- --noplot --sample-size 20 --measurement-time 1` ✅

Benchmark evidence:
- `RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH mode=strict bench=sos_barrier_size_class_eval_with_lookup ... p50_ns_op=21.400 ...`
- Meets the <30ns target in strict mode for lookup + certificate evaluation.

Traceability:
- Allocator emits structured lifecycle record `event=size_class_certificate` with `requested_size`, `mapped_class_size`, `waste_ratio_ppm`, `cert_value`, `class_membership_valid`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyTiger","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:51.707426224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T02:23:52.423477488Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T02:23:52.423380436Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ste.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2ste","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2tq","title":"EPIC: POSIX Conformance Test Suite","description":"Goal: Full POSIX conformance test suite for all implemented functionality.

Background:
Current conformance testing uses fixture-based comparison against host glibc.
Need to expand to cover POSIX specification requirements directly.

Test Categories:
1. Behavioral conformance (same outputs as spec)
2. Error code conformance (correct errno values)
3. Edge case conformance (boundary conditions per spec)
4. Concurrency conformance (thread-safety requirements)
5. Signal conformance (async-signal-safe functions)

Test Sources:
- POSIX.1-2017 specification (IEEE Std 1003.1)
- glibc test suite (for comparison)
- musl test suite (for reference)
- Custom edge case generators

Coverage Targets:
- All Implemented symbols: 100% behavioral coverage
- All RawSyscall symbols: error path coverage
- Edge cases: at least 5 per function
- Concurrency: race condition tests for thread-safe functions

Infrastructure:
- Fixture capture from multiple libcs (glibc, musl)
- Differential testing framework
- Property-based testing (proptest/quickcheck)
- Spec traceability matrix

Success Criteria:
- Zero behavioral differences vs glibc for covered symbols
- All POSIX-mandated error conditions tested
- Thread safety verified for all MT-Safe functions","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:58:36.719814629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:05:59.380649240Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","posix","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tq","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tq","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":791,"issue_id":"bd-2tq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:07Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2tq.1","title":"POSIX test vector extraction","description":"POSIX.1-2017 test vector extraction and validation.

Goal: Extract test vectors from POSIX specification for conformance testing.

Specification Scope:
- IEEE Std 1003.1-2017 (POSIX.1)
- System Interfaces volume
- All function pages with defined behavior

Extraction Methodology:
1. Parse specification for each function
2. Extract defined behavior clauses
3. Extract error conditions (ERRORS section)
4. Generate test vectors from behavior

Test Vector Categories:
1. Positive cases (normal operation)
2. Boundary cases (limits, edge values)
3. Error cases (each ERRNO condition)
4. Undefined behavior (document, don't test)

Data Structure:
{
  "function": "strlen",
  "spec_section": "string.h",
  "test_vectors": [
    {"input": "hello", "expected_output": 5, "category": "positive"},
    {"input": "", "expected_output": 0, "category": "boundary"},
    {"input": null, "expected_behavior": "undefined", "category": "undefined"}
  ]
}

Coverage Metrics:
- Functions covered / total POSIX functions
- Error conditions tested / total error conditions
- Boundary conditions tested

Success Criteria:
- All Implemented functions have POSIX test vectors
- All ERRORS conditions tested
- Coverage report generated

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:18.406834898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:32.999693865Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","posix"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Categories: 57 positive, 32 boundary, 12 error, 3 undefined documented. Each vector includes spec_section reference, input/expected pairs, and category classification. Validation script at scripts/check_posix_test_vectors.sh passes.  {"id":"bd-2tq.2","title":"Differential testing: glibc/musl/bionic","description":"Differential testing against multiple libc implementations.

Goal: Compare FrankenLibC behavior against glibc, musl, and bionic.

Reference Implementations:
1. glibc (primary reference)
2. musl (alternate reference for clean behavior)
3. bionic (Android, for mobile considerations)

Testing Infrastructure:
1. Docker containers with each libc
2. Common test harness that runs on all
3. Output comparison framework
4. Diff visualization and reporting

Test Categories:
1. Return value comparison
2. errno comparison
3. Side effect comparison (file changes, etc.)
4. Performance comparison (informational)

Differential Classes:
- Match: All implementations agree
- glibc-specific: Only glibc has behavior
- POSIX-compliant: Matches spec, differs from glibc
- FrankenLibC-specific: Our healing/safety behavior

Handling Differences:
- Document each difference
- Classify as: bug, intentional, undefined behavior
- Add to known-differences database

Success Criteria:
- Test suite runs on all reference implementations
- All differences documented and classified
- Zero unintentional differences for Implemented functions

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:29.292968936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:32.772871742Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2tq.3","title":"Property-based testing framework","description":"Property-based testing for algorithmic correctness.

Goal: Use property testing to verify implementation invariants.

Properties by Category:

String Operations:
- strlen(s) == position of first null byte
- strcmp(a, a) == 0 (reflexivity)
- strcmp(a, b) == -strcmp(b, a) (antisymmetry)
- memcpy(dst, src, n); memcmp(dst, src, n) == 0

Allocator:
- malloc(n) returns aligned pointer or null
- malloc(n) followed by free(p) leaves consistent state
- realloc preserves content up to min(old_size, new_size)

Math:
- sin²(x) + cos²(x) ≈ 1
- exp(log(x)) ≈ x for x > 0
- abs(x) >= 0
- copysign(abs(x), y) == copysign(x, y) when x >= 0

Membrane:
- validate(p) after malloc(p) returns Valid
- validate(p) after free(p) returns Freed or Quarantined
- SafetyState lattice join is commutative

Infrastructure:
- proptest crate for Rust
- Custom strategies for libc types
- Shrinking for minimal counterexamples

Success Criteria:
- Properties defined for all major function families
- 10000+ test cases per property
- All properties pass

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:36.502407758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-05T23:49:18.463290304Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","property"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1388,"issue_id":"bd-2tq.3","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"[2026-04-05] Added the missing allocator property slice to `crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/property_tests.rs` instead of leaving allocator coverage out-of-scope. New proptest coverage now checks: (1) `size_class::bin_index` rounds requests up to the minimal covering bin, including the large-allocation sentinel path; (2) `bin_size` round-trips through `bin_index`; (3) `LargeAllocator::alloc` preserves page alignment and accounting invariants; (4) multiple large allocations receive distinct bases with exact total mapping; (5) `LargeAllocator::realloc` keeps a single live allocation and refreshes accounting.

Verification via `rch`:
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_core_property_lavendermarsh cargo test -p frankenlibc-core --test property_tests -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_check_lavendermarsh_prop cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_clippy_lavendermarsh_prop cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check`

This advances the bead, but I am leaving it open: the current shared property suite still does not satisfy the bead-level acceptance language around broader family coverage / membrane properties / 10000+ cases / deterministic evidence plumbing.","created_at":"2026-04-05T23:43:16Z"},{"id":1389,"issue_id":"bd-2tq.3","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"[2026-04-05] Follow-on membrane property audit + implementation. Audit result: the lattice algebra portion of this bead was already covered by existing proptests in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/lattice.rs` (`prop_join_commutative`, associativity/idempotence, absorption, permission monotonicity), and pipeline stage-order invariants were already covered in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/ptr_validator.rs` (`dependency_safe_order_property_holds`).

I added the missing *dynamic* membrane properties directly in `ptr_validator.rs`:
- `allocated_pointer_property_validates_as_live`
- `freed_pointer_property_becomes_temporal_violation`

These generatively check the bead-level `validate(p) after malloc(p)` and `validate(p) after free(p)` expectations across allocation sizes instead of only via single deterministic fixtures.

Verification via `rch`:
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_membrane_ptr_validator_lavendermarsh cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane ptr_validator --lib -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_check_lavendermarsh_membraneprop cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_clippy_lavendermarsh_membraneprop cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check`

Net effect: `bd-2tq.3` now has explicit property coverage in the shared core suite for string/math/conversion/ctype/inet/allocator plus membrane algebra/order/alloc-free transitions. I am still leaving the bead open because the acceptance language around broader family saturation, 10000+ case counts, and deterministic artifact/logging plumbing remains larger than this slice.","created_at":"2026-04-05T23:49:18Z"}]}

Both `crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/property_tests.rs` and `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/ptr_validator.rs` now use an env-configurable proptest config via `FRANKENLIBC_PROPTEST_CASES`, and both disable proptest regression-file persistence (`failure_persistence: None`) so remote `rch` runs stay clean and deterministic instead of emitting `FileFailurePersistence::SourceParallel` noise.

High-case verification now has exact replayable commands:
- `rch exec -- env FRANKENLIBC_PROPTEST_CASES=10000 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_core_property_10k_lavendermarsh cargo test -p frankenlibc-core --test property_tests -- --nocapture`
  Result: `48 passed`, finished test execution in ~`0.66s` after build.
- `rch exec -- env FRANKENLIBC_PROPTEST_CASES=10000 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_membrane_ptr_validator_10k_lavendermarsh cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane ptr_validator --lib -- --nocapture`
  Result: `25 passed`, finished test execution in ~`78.82s` after build; harness emitted the standard `running for over 60 seconds` warning for the two new alloc/free membrane properties before they completed successfully.

Post-change verification via `rch` also passed:
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_check_lavendermarsh_propinfra cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_clippy_lavendermarsh_propinfra cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check`

Net effect: `bd-2tq.3` now has a real high-case verification path, not just ordinary default-count property tests. Remaining scope is still broader family saturation plus artifact/logging packaging, so the bead remains open.","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:06:15Z"},{"id":1391,"issue_id":"bd-2tq.3","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"[2026-04-06] Extended the same env-driven high-case property infrastructure to `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/lattice.rs`, so the membrane algebra slice now shares the `FRANKENLIBC_PROPTEST_CASES` + `failure_persistence: None` path instead of leaving lattice proptests on the default runner behavior.

Additional high-case verification via `rch`:
- `rch exec -- env FRANKENLIBC_PROPTEST_CASES=10000 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_membrane_lattice_10k_lavendermarsh cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane lattice --lib -- --nocapture`
  Result: `29 passed`, test execution ~`0.19s` after build.

Final post-change verification for the current tree also passed via `rch`:
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_check_lavendermarsh_latticeinfra cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_clippy_lavendermarsh_latticeinfra cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check`

At this point the bead has explicit 10k-case evidence for core property tests, membrane lattice algebra, and membrane ptr-validator alloc/free properties, with timings recorded for the cheap and expensive slices.","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:10:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2tq.4","title":"POSIX obligation matrix + test traceability mapping","description":"Background:
- POSIX conformance requires explicit traceability from test cases to standard obligations.

Scope:
- Build machine-readable POSIX obligation matrix and map it to symbol families and fixture packs.
- Include mandatory error-condition obligations and async/concurrency constraints.

Deliverables:
1) POSIX obligation matrix.
2) Mapping from obligations to tests/artifacts.
3) Gap report for uncovered obligations.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every covered obligation has at least one deterministic test mapping.
- Uncovered obligations are explicitly tracked with owners.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for matrix parser/join logic.
- E2E generation of obligation coverage report.
- Logs: trace_id, posix_ref, symbol_family, coverage_state, test_refs.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"2026-03-13 GoldSquirrel: completed the machine-readable POSIX obligation matrix/report, traceability JSON/markdown integration, deterministic gate script, and canonical artifact. Artifacts: tests/conformance/posix_obligation_matrix.v1.json; target/conformance/posix_obligation_matrix.current.v1.json; target/conformance/traceability_matrix.current.md; target/conformance/traceability_matrix.current.json; target/conformance/posix_obligation_matrix.log.jsonl. Verification via rch: cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test posix_traceability_test -- --nocapture PASS; cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --test posix_traceability_test -- -D warnings PASS. Workspace clippy still fails on the pre-existing unrelated lint at crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/property_tests.rs:455. cargo fmt --check still fails on pre-existing unrelated formatting drift outside this bead.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldSquirrel","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.255685238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T00:02:03.565774068Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T00:02:03.565035254Z","close_reason":"Implemented POSIX obligation matrix + traceability mapping with canonical gate/tests; verified bead scope through rch. Repo-wide clippy/fmt still have unrelated pre-existing failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","posix","traceability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2tq.5","title":"Errno + edge-case conformance expansion for high-impact APIs","description":"Background:
- Error-code and edge-path mismatches are a common compatibility failure class.

Scope:
- Expand deterministic tests for errno correctness, boundary conditions, and corner-case semantics across implemented/raw families.
- Prioritize high-impact APIs from workload traces.

Deliverables:
1) Errno/edge-case fixture packs.
2) Differential expectation tables.
3) Triage templates for mismatch diagnosis.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Declared high-priority APIs have explicit errno/edge coverage.
- Mismatch reports include actionable detail.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for expectation evaluators.
- E2E conformance pack execution in strict/hardened where relevant.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, case_id, expected_errno, actual_errno, diff_ref.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"Expanded pthread/cond executor coverage in crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs to cover pthread_cond_init/destroy/wait/timedwait/signal/broadcast plus deterministic fixture roundtrip coverage from tests/conformance/fixtures/pthread_cond.json. Verification: rch check; cargo check -p frankenlibc_conformance --all-targets; cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance pthread_cond -- --nocapture; cargo clippy -p frankenlibc_conformance --tests -- -D warnings; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; bash scripts/check_errno_edge_report.sh. Workspace clippy still fails on the pre-existing manual_range_contains lint in crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/property_tests.rs:455. cargo fmt --check still fails on pre-existing unrelated formatting drift across frankenlibc-abi and frankenlibc-membrane files.

Refreshed the canonical conformance/errno-edge evidence to match the now-implemented pthread_cond family: tests/conformance/conformance_matrix.v1.json now records 663 total cases with 457 passes / 206 errors, and tests/conformance/errno_edge_report.v1.json now records 620 tracked edge cases with 421 covered / 199 failing. Added regression coverage in crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/conformance_matrix_test.rs and crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/errno_edge_report_test.rs that pins the canonical pthread_cond rows away from unsupported_function and requires pass/covered status. Verification for this slice: rch check; cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test conformance_matrix_test -- --nocapture; cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test errno_edge_report_test -- --nocapture; bash scripts/check_conformance_matrix.sh; bash scripts/check_errno_edge_report.sh; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --tests -- -D warnings. Full workspace clippy still fails on the pre-existing manual_range_contains lint in crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/property_tests.rs:455. cargo fmt --check still fails on pre-existing unrelated formatting drift across frankenlibc-abi tests and frankenlibc-membrane sources/tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldSquirrel","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.392357602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:25:25.450873591Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["errno","posix","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2tq.6","title":"Concurrency/signal POSIX suites + differential multi-libc runner","description":"Background:
- Concurrency and signal semantics are critical for POSIX credibility.

Scope:
- Implement dedicated conformance packs for MT-safe behavior, synchronization semantics, and signal interaction requirements.
- Add differential mode comparing glibc/musl/frankenlibc outputs where feasible.

Deliverables:
1) Concurrency/signal conformance suites.
2) Differential runner and report format.
3) POSIX gate integration.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Core MT/signal obligations have deterministic pass/fail evidence.
- Differential report highlights semantic drifts explicitly.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for differential comparator.
- E2E multi-libc conformance runs.
- Logs: trace_id, suite, libc_variant, mode, result, drift_class.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.524481767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:17:06.317324822Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["posix","pthread","signal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.6","depends_on_id":"bd-15n.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
